items.headline: Major electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive research campus
items.summary: A significant electrical infrastructure project has been permitted at 3210 Porter Drive to support new R&D tenant operations. The work includes installation of new transformers and switches with conduit extensions to a substation on Hanover Drive, indicating substantial expansion of research and development activity in Palo Alto's innovation corridor.

items.headline: Historic house preserved while four townhomes added behind it
items.summary: A developer is seeking approval to keep the original 960-square foot historic home at 434 Crescent Avenue while building four three-story townhomes on the rear of the property. The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing the proposal to determine if the plan adequately protects the existing historic resource.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: The City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement activities. This policy protects undocumented residents from immigration enforcement actions on city grounds.

items.headline: City approves digital advertising deal with Clear Channel on public property
items.summary: San José amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC to place large format digital signage on city-owned property. This expands advertising on public assets and could affect the visual landscape of various city locations.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The City Council reviewed a report on requests for proposals to contract cleanup services for encampments. This indicates the city is actively addressing homeless encampment management through contracted services.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations to buy or lease the property at 10480 Finch Avenue from the Cupertino Union School District, with price and payment terms still being worked out. This could signal a future city project or development on that site, though details remain confidential during negotiations.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area, which will guide future recreational development and connectivity in that part of Milpitas. This plan shapes what parks and outdoor spaces residents will have access to over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council updates internal procedures and protocols handbook
items.summary: The council approved changes to how it conducts meetings and operates, though the specific changes are not detailed in the agenda summary. This affects how city business is conducted and potentially how residents can participate in council proceedings.
